notes: Ran the entire game, beginning to end, without a single glitch or issue of any kind.
title: Castlevania: Lords of Shadow 2

notes: Works with high settings. Only issue found was when alt tabbing or switching desktops. After maximizing back to full screen the game scene sometimes turns black, but HUD is still displayed. Toying around didn't do any success so don't alt tab during gameplay, or be prepared to restart it.
title: Castlevania: Lords of Shadow 2

notes: Works perfectly out of the box, with controller and in 1440p 60fps everything maxed out. At the time of writing this game is borked (at least for me) with Proton 4.2-9 but as already said, platinum with older Proton version.
title: Castlevania: Lords of Shadow 2

notes:
Disable esync
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1

Force DX11
PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%

Disable DX11
PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%

Force DX10
PROTON_USE_WINED3D10=1 %command%

Disable DX10
PROTON_NO_D3D10=1 %command%

these should be used on launch options :)
title: Castlevania: Lords of Shadow 2

notes:
Running max settings at 2560*1600, the game does not support ultra-widescreen but that is not a Linux issue. Performance under defaults was problematic. Light sources rendered as solid colours and stuttering and freezing occurred often. Using "PROTON_USE_D3D11=1 %command%" to force the DX11 DXVK code-path instead of earlier DX fallback resolves some graphical issues but using regular Wine with "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" was needed to fully resolve these issues.  
Controller seems unresponsive but I think this is one of those games where you have to act at a particular point in the characters animation loop.
title: Castlevania: Lords of Shadow 2
